_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-27 Quality Improvement Program (Revised 6/30/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-27: Quality Improvement Program — MDHHS Approved 6/30/2023
purpose: Define the required components of an MCA Quality Improvement (QI) program. All MCAs must have a functioning QI program. QI programs are designed to improve patient care, identify system deficiencies, ensure protocol compliance, and support provider education. QI data is used to inform protocol revisions and system-level improvements.

sections.title: Required QI Program Components
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-27: QI Program — Required Components
points: All MCAs must have an active QI program with the following required components:
— Written QI plan with goals, metrics, and review cycle
— MCA Medical Director involvement and oversight
— PCR/ePCR review process for all or a statistically valid sample of calls
— Mandatory review categories: cardiac arrest outcomes (ROSC, TOR), 12-lead ECG acquisition and STEMI notification, airway management (advanced airway, pediatric airway), stroke scale documentation
— Mechanism for provider feedback based on QI findings
— Tracking of protocol deviations and adverse events

sections.title: Mandatory 100% QA/QI Review
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-27: QI Program — Mandatory 100% Review
points: 100% review required for all of the following — as specified in applicable protocols:
— All pediatric advanced airway placements (Section 7-11: Airway Management)
— All double sequential defibrillation uses (Section 7-9: Double Sequential Defibrillation)
— All cases where dead on scene criteria are applied (Section 7-6)

sections.title: QI Feedback and Provider Education
points: QI programs must have a mechanism to provide individual provider feedback on performance
Aggregate QI data (de-identified) must be shared with EMS agencies and providers within the MCA
QI findings that identify systemic issues must be addressed with targeted education and protocol revisions as appropriate
Providers are expected to participate in QI feedback processes and incorporate QI-identified improvements into practice

sections.title: Reporting to MDHHS
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-27: QI Program — MDHHS Reporting
points: MCAs must submit QI program reports to MDHHS per MDHHS requirements
MDHHS may use aggregate QI data for statewide EMS system performance evaluation and protocol development

scope_notes: MCAs must have active QI program with written plan, Medical Director oversight, PCR review, feedback mechanism
Mandatory QI categories: cardiac arrest outcomes, 12-lead/STEMI, airway management, stroke documentation
100% review required: pediatric advanced airways, cricothyroidotomies, double sequential defibrillation, protocol deviations, DOA applications
Provider feedback: individual and aggregate de-identified data sharing
MCAs report to MDHHS per MDHHS requirements
